basics.name: Michael Doyle
basics.label: AI Researcher & Engineer
basics.summary: An AI researcher with a passion for language, open source, and applying research to important problems.

work.name: The MITRE Corporation
work.summary: Led multiple ML projects. Researched, developed, tested, trained, and deployed machine learning models and applications.
highlights: Led research project on LLMs, IT Modernization, and code understanding
Led research project on training object detectors for neuromorphic cameras
Maintained internal GPU servers and services on OpenShift and Linux servers
Developed and open sourced fire simulator to be used in RL training for wildfire mitigation
Developed and open sourced LLM IT modernization library
Developed agentic LLM backend prototype for government sponsor application
Researched adversarial object detection and classification
Researched adversarial vision for classical depth
Researched adversarial text for Machine Translation
Directly deployed trained models on government sponsor systems
Engaged in government sponsor test events in the field

work.name: Space Micro Inc.
work.summary: Wrote, simulated, synthesized, and implemented FPGA code in VHDL using ModelSim, Synplify Pro, Vivado, and Libero Designer. Designed PCBs in KiCAD and tested hardware.

publications.name: Testing the Effect of Code Documentation on Large Language Model Code Understanding
publications.publisher: North American Association for Computational Linguistics (NAACL)
publications.summary: Empirically analyzes how code documentation quality impacts the code generation and understanding capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). It reveals that incorrect documentation significantly hinders LLMs' code comprehension, while incomplete or missing documentation has no significant impact.

publications.name: The vulnerability of UAVs: an adversarial machine learning perspective
publications.summary: Proposes a methodology to evaluate the vulnerability of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to adversarial machine learning attacks by analyzing potential attack vectors at each stage of UAV operation.

publications.name: Reinforcement Learning for Wildfire Mitigation in Simulated Disaster Environments
publications.summary: Presents a reinforcement learning approach to wildfire mitigation in simulated disaster environments. We release two software libraries, SimFire and SimHarness, to facilitate future research in this area.

publications.name: Practical Attacks on Machine Translation using Paraphrase
publications.publisher: Association for Machine Translation in the Americas (AMTA)
publications.summary: Investigated the vulnerability of machine translation systems to adversarial attacks constructed with limited information. A novel attack method was proposed that generates perturbations using paraphrases and evaluates their impact on meaning preservation and translation degradation across various language pairs and systems.

projects.name: SimFire
projects.summary: A wildfire simulator written in Python and meant for Reinforcement Learning research for wildfire mitigation.

projects.name: Janus LLM
projects.summary: A library for LLM IT modernization, using LLMs, RAG, and intelligent chunking.

projects.name: SimHarness
projects.summary: A reinforcement learning harness meant to be used with SimFire for wildfire mitigation research.
